zh.lifeSummary: 當William Jackson於1545年出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Thomas Jacson26歲，母親Joan Cornwell24歲。他於1570年與Elizabeth Wytham在英國艾可特林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Jackson于1545年出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲Thomas Jacson26岁，母亲Joan Cornwell24岁。他于1570年与Elizabeth Wytham在英国艾可特林结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Jackson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: patronymic from Jack . In North America, this surname has absorbed other patronymics beginning with J- in various European languages, in particular those derived from equivalents or short forms and other derivatives of the personal name Jacob , e.g. Norwegian Jacobsen or Jakobsen and, in some cases, Slovenian Jakše (from a derivative of the personal name Jakob ). This surname is also very common among African Americans (see also 2 below).

African American: from the personal name Jackson (or Andrew Jackson), adopted in honor of Andrew Jackson, the 7th president of the US; or adoption of the surname in 1 above, in many cases probably for the same reason.

History: This extremely common British name was brought over by numerous different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One forebear was the father and namesake of the seventh US president, Andrew Jackson, who migrated to SC from Carrickfergus in the north of Ireland in 1765. The Confederate General Thomas ‘Stonewall’ Jackson came from VA, where his great-grandfather John, likewise of Scotch–Irish stock, had settled after emigrating to America in 1748.

variants.name: Jack
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English and Older Scots personal name Jak, Jakke, Jagge, Jake, Jeke, Jegge, a Picard-Flemish denasalized form of Old Picard and Middle Dutch Janke, a pet form of Jan (see John ). It was introduced by Flemings and Picards into Norman and Anglo-Norman usage, whence it became a common English and Scottish pet form of John. Although the surname is mainly Scottish in distribution, it also occurs in England, though the more common form there is Jackson .

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English borrowing of the Old French personal name Jacque(s) (James). However, it is uncertain whether English Jack was ever used as an alternative to James.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Jack (see 1 above) as a surname.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Jackson was born in 1545, in Essex, England, his father, Thomas Jacson, was 26 and his mother, Joan Cornwell, was 24. He married Elizabeth Wytham in 1570, in Terling, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Jackson est né en 1545, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Jacson, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joan Cornwell, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Wytham en 1570, à Terling, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Jackson 1545 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Jacson 26 und seine Mutter Joan Cornwell 24. Er hat 1570 in Terling, Essex, England Elizabeth Wytham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Jackson è nato il nel 1545 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, Thomas Jacson, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Joan Cornwell, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Wytham nel 1570, nel Terling, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: William Jacksonが1545年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Thomas Jacsonは26歳，母Joan Cornwellは24歳であった。1570年にイギリスエセックステルリングでElizabeth Wythamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: William Jackson(이)가 1545년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Jacson(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Cornwell(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1570년 영국 에세스 털링에서 Elizabeth Wytham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Jackson nasceu em 1545, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Jacson, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Joan Cornwell, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Wytham em 1570, em Terling, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Jackson родился в 1545 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Jacson было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Cornwell, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Wytham в 1570 г. в местности тирлинг, эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Jackson nació en 1545, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Jacson, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Joan Cornwell, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Wytham en 1570, en Terling, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas.
